sections.paragraph: m. a magian, the name of the priests and wise men among the Medes, Persians, and Babylonians, prop. great, powerful. (To this answer the Pers. مغ mogh, magian, from mh mih, great, leader; Zend. meh, má, m̂o; Sansc. mahat, maĥ, in which is found the root of the Gr. μέγαζ, and Lat. magis, magnus.) רַב־מָג by apposition, prince magian, chief of the magi, Jer 39:3.
